issue_description: Phases without dependencies on each other should execute concurrently. Currently, the sequencer runs phases one-at-a-time in topological order. With parallel execution, independent phases at the same topological depth run simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria: Phases at the same topological depth (wave) execute concurrently using threading
New `parallel` field on PipelineTemplate (default: true) — set to false for sequential
New `max_parallel` field on PipelineTemplate (default: 0 = unlimited) — limits concurrent phases
Error in one parallel phase: `fail_fast` field (default: true) — aborts siblings if true, lets them finish if false
Token/cost tracking aggregates correctly across parallel phases
Heartbeat module works correctly with parallel phases (show all active phases)
All existing sequential tests still pass unchanged
New tests for: parallel execution, max_parallel limiting, fail_fast both modes, error handling
codebase_context: The sequencer (src/orchestration_engine/sequencer.py, ~31KB) already computes waves via Kahn's algorithm in TemplateEngine.get_execution_order(). The inner loop at line 99 says 'MVP: sequential within each wave (no actual parallelism)'. The fix is to use concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor to run phases within each wave in parallel. PipelineTemplate (src/orchestration_engine/templates.py) needs 3 new fields: parallel (bool), max_parallel (int), fail_fast (bool). The YAML loader in _load_template_data() needs to parse these. PhaseSequencer._execute_and_wait() is the per-phase execution method. The heartbeat module (src/orchestration_engine/heartbeat.py) tracks current_phase — needs to handle multiple concurrent phases.
files_to_modify: src/orchestration_engine/sequencer.py — add ThreadPoolExecutor parallel execution in wave loop
src/orchestration_engine/templates.py — add parallel, max_parallel, fail_fast fields to PipelineTemplate and YAML loading
src/orchestration_engine/heartbeat.py — support multiple concurrent phases display
constraints: Use only stdlib (concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor) — no new dependencies
Backward compatible — existing templates without parallel field default to parallel=true
Thread safety: phase_outputs dict must be protected with a lock
Progress callbacks must be thread-safe (use a lock)
The runner.queue operations (submit_task, poll, update) are already thread-safe (SQLite WAL mode)
Keep the existing sequential path working when parallel=false
